label: Spotify
description: Access Spotify's music catalog, user library, playlists, and listening history via the Spotify Web API.

form.title: Connect Spotify
form.subtitle: Authorize Anton to read your Spotify library, playlists, and listening history.

methods.label: Sign in with Spotify (OAuth 2.0)
methods.description: OAuth 2.0 via a Spotify Developer app you register. Paste your Client ID and Client Secret, then Anton opens a browser consent screen to obtain a scoped access token. No passwords stored.
methods.how_to:
## Register a Spotify Developer App

Spotify requires you to create an app on their developer dashboard to obtain OAuth credentials.

1. Go to [developer.spotify.com/dashboard](https://developer.spotify.com/dashboard) and log in with your Spotify account.
2. Click **Create app**.
3. Fill in an **App name** (e.g. `Anton`) and **App description** (any text).
4. Set **Redirect URI** to `http://127.0.0.1:9898/callback` — Anton's local redirect handler.
5. Under **APIs used**, select **Web API**, then click **Save**.
6. Open the app you just created and click **Settings**.
7. Copy the **Client ID** and click **View client secret** to reveal and copy the **Client secret**.
8. Paste both into the fields below and click **Submit**. A browser window will open to Spotify's consent screen.
9. Log in (if not already) and click **Agree** to grant the requested permissions.

### Scopes requested

- `user-library-read` — saved tracks, albums, and shows
- `playlist-read-private` + `playlist-read-collaborative` — all playlists
- `user-read-recently-played` — listening history
- `user-top-read` — top artists and tracks
- `user-read-email` — your Spotify email address

### Notes

- Apps in **Development mode** allow up to 25 Spotify users. For broader access, apply for **Extended Quota** in the dashboard.
- Spotify access tokens expire after 1 hour; Anton will automatically refresh them using the stored refresh token.
- If you need to revoke access, visit [spotify.com/account/apps](https://www.spotify.com/account/apps) and click **Remove Access** next to your app.

fields.name: client_id
fields.label: Client ID
fields.description: From your Spotify Developer Dashboard → your app → Settings → Client ID.

fields.name: client_secret
fields.label: Client Secret
fields.description: From your Spotify Developer Dashboard → your app → Settings → View client secret.

fields.name: redirect_uri
fields.label: Redirect URI
fields.description: Must exactly match the Redirect URI you entered in your Spotify app settings.

methods.label: Client Credentials (catalog only)
methods.description: App-level access to Spotify's public music catalog — no user login required. Suitable for searching tracks, albums, and artists, but cannot access any personal library or playlist data.
methods.how_to:
## Client Credentials Flow

Use this method when you only need access to Spotify's public catalog (search, album info, artist data) and do not need any user-specific data.

1. Go to [developer.spotify.com/dashboard](https://developer.spotify.com/dashboard) and create an app (same steps as OAuth above, but no Redirect URI is needed).
2. Open your app → **Settings** and copy the **Client ID** and **Client secret**.
3. Paste both into the fields below and click **Submit**.

### What you can access

- Track, album, artist, and playlist metadata (public only)
- Search across Spotify's full catalog
- Browse featured playlists, new releases, and categories

### What you cannot access

- Saved library, listening history, or private playlists — use **Sign in with Spotify** for those.

### Notes

- Tokens expire after 1 hour; Anton refreshes automatically.
- No user consent screen is shown — this is purely app-to-app authentication.
